name: Academic Document Design & Formatting
description: Applies academic design themes, color palettes, typography rules, heading styles, and professional formatting to thesis, research papers, and journal articles. Ensures consistent academic presentation across all chapters.
ethical_boundary: Do not modify content, claims, citations, or data. Only apply formatting and design rules. Respect the author's chosen style and institution requirements.

rules.description: Ensure heading levels follow consistent hierarchy: H1 (chapter titles), H2 (major sections), H3 (subsections), H4 (sub-subsections). No skipped levels.
Body text should use serif fonts (Georgia, Times New Roman) at 11-12pt. Headings may use sans-serif (Arial, Helvetica). Monospace for code/technical content.

Apply the selected color theme consistently: accent color for headings, muted background for tables/figures, neutral for body text. Available themes: Classic Blue (#1F4E79), Emerald Academic (#0F766E), Mono Formal (#111827), Maroon Submission (#7F1D1D), Royal Purple (#5B21B6).
Body text: 1.5 or double line spacing. Margins: 1 inch (2.54cm) all sides for thesis, 1 inch for research papers. Block quotes: indented 0.5 inch, single-spaced.
Tables: numbered sequentially, title above, notes below. Figures: numbered sequentially, caption below. Both: centered, clear labels, consistent styling.
Front matter (TOC, LOT, LOF, Abstract): roman numerals (i, ii, iii...). Main body: arabic numerals starting from 1. Landscape pages: number placement consistent with portrait.
All in-text citations and reference list entries must follow the chosen style (APA 7, Harvard, IEEE, UGC thesis format, etc.) consistently. No mixing of styles.
Auto-generate Table of Contents, List of Tables, and List of Figures with accurate page numbers. Update on every export.

Auto-detect chapters that benefit from diagrams (methodology, framework, process descriptions). Do NOT add diagrams to Abstract, Introduction, Conclusion, or Acknowledgements. Insert diagrams near their first reference in the text.
Chapter titles: bold, 16-18pt, accent color. Section headings: bold, 14pt. Subsection: bold-italic, 12pt. Consistent spacing before/after each heading level.

design_check.template: Review the following academic chapter for formatting consistency. Check: heading hierarchy, font usage, spacing, table/figure formatting, and citation style. Return a JSON object with 'issues' (array of formatting problems found) and 'score' (0-100 formatting quality). Do NOT modify content, only assess formatting.
